characterCraft.note: At 160x360 the 13x21 rigger keeps a helmet, face, backpack, facing, separate limbs, gait, climbing, bracing, and hurt poses. Four smaller workers retain trade-specific helmets and tools, individual silhouettes, gait and panic poses, matching MACHINE HUNT's micro-character clarity and BLOCK MINE's role identity without enlarging the cast.

environmentCraft.note: The strip reads as a built industrial tower with riveted decks, cross-bracing, usable and broken ladders, catwalk gaps, pipes, tanks, lamps, pistons, foreground steel, skyline depth, and district landmarks. animationImpact.note: Aligned real-pixel bursts verify climbing limbs, a worker joining the convoy, barrel rotation and travel, while the live route redraw, 240-frame warning column, cascading hazards, panic reactions, cage descent, broad confetti, hitstop and slow motion give signature beats anticipation and follow-through at the reference floor.

readability.note: The mint forecast line, pulsing next junction, coral abandoned line, amber purge route, full-width barrel runway, helmet colors, convoy HUD, and rooftop cage remain distinguishable at native size. Actor footprints stay near six to seven percent in sampled play, leaving substantially more world and approach room than the scale-law maximum.

artDirectionCohesion.note: Steel values, safety yellow, mint planning, coral danger, helmet accents, signage, industrial HUD panels, hazard stripes, particles, and landmark lighting share one authored rescue-tower grammar across every district.
